title: Roadmap for the migration to post-quantum cryptography for the Government of Canada (ITSM.40.001)
summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security's recommended roadmap for the Government of Canada to migrate non-classified IT systems to post-quantum cryptography.

title: Preparing your organization for the quantum threat to cryptography (ITSAP.00.017)
summary: Cyber Centre guidance explaining post-quantum cryptography as algorithms designed to be quantum-safe while running on conventional computers, and advising organizations on preparing for the quantum threat.

title: Cyber Centre's summary review of final candidates for NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography standards
summary: Summary review by Cyber Centre cryptographic scientists evaluating the remaining finalist and alternate candidates in the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography standardization process.

title: GC Migration to Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) - Course CRY102S
summary: Online training course (CRY102S) from the Cyber Centre's Learning Hub presenting the Government of Canada's plan for migrating to post-quantum cryptography.

title: Migration to post-quantum cryptography for the Government of Canada (policy implementation notice)
summary: Government of Canada digital policy implementation notice on migrating government systems to post-quantum cryptography, referencing the Cyber Centre's migration roadmap.

title: Looking ahead: Quantum computing and cybersecurity
summary: Shared Services Canada campaign article on quantum computing and cybersecurity, discussing post-quantum cryptography and citing the Communications Security Establishment's assessment of the quantum threat.

title: National Quantum Strategy Roadmap: Quantum communication and post-quantum cryptography
summary: Roadmap under Canada's National Quantum Strategy addressing quantum communication and post-quantum cryptography as responses to the threat posed by cryptographically relevant quantum computers.

title: Canadian National Quantum-Readiness: Best Practices
summary: Quantum-readiness best practices document (v04, July 2024) published via ISED, addressing preparation for quantum-safe post-quantum cryptography while noting that quantum-resistant solutions were not yet fully available.

title: Quantum Safe Technologies Initiative
summary: National Research Council of Canada programme supporting the testing and integration of post-quantum cryptography into critical systems and building Canadian capacity through training.

title: Cryptography and quantum computing (technical advisory services)
summary: NRC technical advisory services page on the design, development and implementation of cryptographic solutions and quantum computing; the candidate description does not explicitly name post-quantum cryptography.

title: Recommended Contract Clauses for Cryptography (ITSM.00.501)
summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security guidance (September 2025) providing recommended procurement contract clauses for cryptographic products, explicitly requiring that cryptographic modules support appropriate post-quantum cryptography (per ITSP.40.111) for key establishment and digital signatures by the end of 2026. Emphasises cryptographic agility to ease the PQC migration.

title: Cryptographic algorithms for UNCLASSIFIED, PROTECTED A, and PROTECTED B information (ITSP.40.111)
summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security standard (Version 5, effective 29 May 2026) listing approved cryptographic algorithms, now explicitly recommending the NIST-standardised post-quantum algorithms ML-KEM for key establishment and ML-DSA and SLH-DSA for digital signatures. It is the referenced algorithm baseline for the Government of Canada's PQC migration.

title: G7 Cybersecurity Working Group Statement on preparing for a post-quantum cryptography migration
summary: Statement published on Canada's Cyber Centre site (last modified June 2026), co-issued by Canada's Communications Security Establishment with the cyber agencies of the other G7 nations and the EU, offering practical recommendations for medium-to-large organisations to prepare for the transition to post-quantum cryptography. Flagged for review because it is a multinational G7 collective statement rather than a purely domestic Canadian instrument.
